[LW05-004] At Chapter 12's end, the reader can name one dominant new pressure that clearly governs the next chapter's first move, rather than holding three coequal unresolved threads.
[LW06-002] A reader moving from chapter 11 into chapter 12 experiences the immediate consequence of "watch, don't report" on-page rather than learning in summary that the watch already failed.
[length_chapter_12_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.problem: Becky's terrace confession (line 6607: 'He didn't want me. He wanted the version that gets you into rooms') sits between the recognition detonation (Damian identifying Anya) and the missing-courier alert (line 6667). The transitions between these three crises are smooth rather than whiplash-inducing. The tonal contrast between Becky's genuine human vulnerability and the operational emergency could hit harder if the shift were more abrupt -- if the pager vibrated during the most exposed moment of the confession rather than after it has settled, and if Anya's inability to be fully present for her best friend were made more viscerally costly.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6638-6670. Move the second pager alert (line 6667) earlier so it arrives during the peak of Becky's emotional confession rather than after. Let Anya feel the pager vibrate while Becky is saying the thing that matters most, and force the reader to experience Anya's split attention as a betrayal of friendship rather than an operational inconvenience. The goal is tonal whiplash that makes the reader feel what it costs Anya to be a spy who can hear everyone's pain and must leave anyway.

findings.problem: After Becky's confession and the second pager alert, the chapter extends 25+ lines with Anya staring into the dark garden and the narrator restating her entrapment across three simultaneous crises (Damian's recognition, the missing courier, Becky's trust). The chapter's natural close is the pager vibrating during or immediately after the most vulnerable moment of Becky's confession. The staring-into-dark coda converts the triple crisis into a summarized state rather than leaving it as active, unresolved pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6667-6691. End the chapter on the pager vibration and one concrete physical response (Anya's hand tightening on the railing, or her shoulder still pressed against Becky's, or the garden lights going out). Cut the extended meditation on the triple crisis. The reader already knows what's colliding; the chapter's job is to leave them in the collision, not to summarize it.

findings.problem: Becky's terrace confession hits every emotional note perfectly: the breakup with Marc, the documentary dream, the Tuesday-night cereal, the 'you're my best friend' declaration. Each beat escalates in vulnerability with grammatically composed precision. Nothing tangles, nothing arrives in the wrong order, no beat is wasted. A real confession from someone who has been performing all night would be less organized -- she might circle back to a detail she already mentioned, lose the thread, say the wrong thing first, or accidentally reveal something she meant to keep.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6607-6648. Disorder the confession. Let Becky start with the wrong detail (the cat, maybe, or the Tuesday cereal) before she gets to Marc. Let her say 'you're my best friend' too early, before the build has earned it, and then have to circle back to the reason. Let at least one moment in the confession feel like a wrong turn that she has to correct. The emotional arc should still arrive at the same destination, but the path should feel messier, more like someone who has never said these things before rather than someone delivering a perfectly ordered monologue.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch12's venue arrival does not re-inventory chandelier, white table coverings, and bar crowd.
findings.evidence: Ch2 L392: 'chandeliers... White linens glowed under crystal'; Ch12 L6336: 'a chandelier heavy enough to go on a threat map... Tables with white cloths ran along one wall. The buffet ran along the other. The bar already had the thickest crowd'
findings.problem: The Blackbell estate ballroom at Ch12 L6336 is the fifth reception venue. Chandelier (third explicit description), white table coverings (second after Ch2), and bar-as-crowd-magnet (second after Ch8) are recycled venue elements. The underlying inventory repeats even when individual phrases are fresh.
findings.rewrite_direction: Enter the Blackbell ballroom through Becky's personality stamped on the space, the Eden-reunion atmosphere, or the social tension. Cut the chandelier/white-cloths/bar inventory.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch12 contains at most one diplomatic-mask/polish reference rather than three.
findings.evidence: Ch12 L6127: 'Damian's voice came on stripped of its diplomatic polish'; Ch12 L6456: 'Smile at the wall. Say we all look young' (performing at party); Ch12 L6528: 'His face stayed perfectly diplomatic. The thought under it did not'
findings.problem: Damian's diplomatic-mask/polish baseline is proved three times within Ch12 alone (L6127, L6456, L6528). The mask-vs-reality contrast has been independently proved in 7 chapters (Ch2, Ch4, Ch5, Ch7, Ch8, Ch9, Ch12). Ch12's local cluster of three instances in one chapter is the densest accumulation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one diplomatic-mask reference in Ch12 (L6528 is strongest because it pairs face and thought). Cut or vary L6127 and L6456 to avoid re-proving the reader already knows Damian performs in public.

findings.acceptance_test: When read back-to-back, Chapters 10 through 12 no longer all begin with the same abstract elapsed-time recap shape; at least one of the later openings drops the reader directly into a live beat carrying pressure from the prior chapter.
findings.evidence: Three consecutive chapters open by first summarizing elapsed time and offpage action before settling into the live scene: "The next evening" in Chapter 10, "After the canal" across three HQ days in Chapter 11, and "Fourteen hours earlier" with the 0600 watch result in Chapter 12: snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5580; snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5871; snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6250

evidence.excerpt: 5580: Under the kitchen light, the milk was going to be a problem. The next evening, Yor knew only that the milk was going to be a problem.
5871: After the canal, Anya spent three days inside WISE headquarters, and by now the fluorescent buzz felt lodged behind her eyes.

6250: Fourteen hours earlier Anya had been at a WISE listening desk waiting for the 0600 watch on Falk's diplomatic procurement lane to prove she wasn't chasing smoke. It had proved the opposite kind of thing: the coverage cut was real, the desk went blind on schedule, and whatever was supposed to move through it never showed its face. No courier. No pickup. No useful mistake. By noon the route had been salvaged north. A WISE courier was carrying copied procurement papers toward Kreuzfeld on forged customs documents and was supposed to clear the checkpoint and check in from the west side by 2300. Yor had taken the extra night Anya had bought and stayed silent through the day. The watch was over. The damage wasn't.

findings.problem: Across the cluster, the reader keeps being asked to restart instead of ride pressure forward. The repeated time-jump-plus-catch-up opening shape makes the middle window feel modular and backfilled, which dulls the sense that these chapters are happening under one tightening clock.
findings.rewrite_direction: Break the pattern by dramatizing one of the bridge beats in scene, most obviously the 0600 watch, or by entering Chapter 12 through an action already underway instead of another retrospective reset paragraph.

findings.acceptance_test: At Chapter 12's end, the reader can name one dominant new pressure that clearly governs the next chapter's first move, rather than holding three coequal unresolved threads.
findings.evidence: Chapter 12 detonates Damian's recognition at the yearbook table, immediately adds the missed Kreuzfeld courier alert, then diverts into Becky's long terrace confession before Anya takes any concrete action on either crisis: snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6460; snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6518; snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6540; snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6586; snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6644

evidence.excerpt: 6460: "Wait." His whole face lit with the loose clarity alcohol stripped him down to. "This is the funniest possible thing. Becky has seriously been throwing the same event her whole life." He jabbed the page at Damian. "Look. That's you, that's me, that's Becky, and that's Anya Forger pretending not to murder us over raffle tickets. Same face and everything."

6540: Damian was still inside, at the edge of her range. The careful rhythm of his thoughts came through thinned by glass and distance. They had narrowed into two problems he was refusing to let touch. *Not here. Not tonight. If Kreuzfeld caught the courier, the papers are gone.*
6586: "Can I tell you one real thing?" Becky asked.

6644: In another minute Anya would have to go back inside, make an excuse, and disappear into secure traffic that would run the rest of the night. For now she kept her shoulder where it was and looked out into the dark until the garden lights blurred.

findings.problem: The chapter lands three strong pressures at once, but they do not consolidate into a distinct end-state for the window. Recognition, border failure, and Becky intimacy all matter, yet because none is allowed to win the chapter's final leverage, the window ends in suspended triage rather than a sharper changed condition.
findings.rewrite_direction: After Becky's scene, choose a dominant terminal beat: either let Anya take one concrete operational step on the missed courier, or let Damian intercept her and force the recognition thread into a new leverage state before the chapter closes.

findings.acceptance_test: A reader moving from chapter 11 into chapter 12 experiences the immediate consequence of "watch, don't report" on-page rather than learning in summary that the watch already failed.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6227; snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6250; Chapter 11 ends by pinning the next move to a 0600 watch on Falk's desk and Anya stays awake counting down to it; but Chapter 12 only reports the watch result retrospectively in one summary paragraph: snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6195; snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6241

evidence.excerpt: 6227: For a moment she stayed on the floor listening to the pipes and the neighbor upstairs moving around in shoes that should have been illegal after nine. Then she pulled the electricity bill fully onto her lap, turned it over, and wrote:

6250: Fourteen hours earlier Anya had been at a WISE listening desk waiting for the 0600 watch on Falk's diplomatic procurement lane to prove she wasn't chasing smoke. It had proved the opposite kind of thing: the coverage cut was real, the desk went blind on schedule, and whatever was supposed to move through it never showed its face. No courier. No pickup. No useful mistake. By noon the route had been salvaged north. A WISE courier was carrying copied procurement papers toward Kreuzfeld on forged customs documents and was supposed to clear the checkpoint and check in from the west side by 2300. Yor had taken the extra night Anya had bought and stayed silent through the day. The watch was over. The damage wasn't.

6195: "Use what the paper can survive," Damian said. "Use what you can watch happen in real time. If you're right about him, he's going to need that desk dark for a reason. If you're wrong again, you burn another innocent person. So don't guess. Sit on the lane and see who uses it."

6241: It was ugly and it was still the calculation. Her mother was somewhere in the city with Dominic's photographs and whatever was left of her faith in Garden. Anya was betting Yor would stay quiet until tomorrow because the other clock had reached six in the morning and a desk inside WISE was about to go blind.

findings.problem: Chapter 11 closes by loading the 0600 Falk watch as the next live test of whether Anya is finally right, then chapter 12 reports that watch in retrospective summary before moving to the gala. Because the boundary promises a scene and then delivers only a recap of failure, the handoff sheds suspense instead of cashing it in.
findings.rewrite_direction: Dramatize a portion of the 0600 Falk watch on-page before the gala material: even a short beat of the desk going blind and Anya realizing the useful moment has passed would preserve momentum. Let the null result force the Kreuzfeld salvage decision in real time rather than reporting it in retrospective summary. If full dramatization would unbalance the chapter, compress to a scene-fragment: the desk, the silence, the decision to go north.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

Audit every revised passage for hedge words ('something,' 'somehow,' 'seemed to,' 'a kind of,' 'almost'). Replace with specifics wherever the referent can be identified. Intentional vagueness is permitted only when a character is genuinely uncertain or a telepathic signal is genuinely fragmentary. The default must be precision, not approximation.

In any revised dialogue exchange of three or more volleys, ensure at least one moment of rhythmic imbalance: a line that lands wrong, a response at the wrong register, a silence where speech was expected, or a sentence the speaker visibly regrets starting. Do not produce perfectly weighted stichomythia where every line advances the theme at the same pace and length.

Once a chapter's dramatic climax has landed, close within 10-15 lines. Do not extend into narratorial meditation, moral processing, or enumeration of what the climax means. End on a physical detail, an unresolved image, or a single unprocessed sensation. The reader carries the meaning; the chapter's job is to leave them in the collision, not to summarize it.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
